reasoning: Xcel Energy reported Q1 2025 earnings on April 24, slightly missing estimates ($0.84 EPS vs $0.91 expected), which caused a two-day post-earnings selloff, dropping the price from ~$69.35 to $66.88 by Friday, April 25. The baseline threshold for this question is the April 27 equivalent (so the April 25 close of roughly $66.88). Short-term momentum is slightly negative due to the earnings reaction, but there are no structural red flags as guidance was reaffirmed. Following the principle that short-term weekly stock moves are highly unpredictable and random, I keep the 1-week probability slightly below 50% (0.48) to reflect the recent negative momentum. Over 1 to 6 months, the momentum effects fade and market drift dominates. Historically, equities have a baseline probability of around 52-54% to be higher over 3-6 month periods. The probabilities gradually increase towards this base rate, remaining close to 0.5 as dictated by the low predictability of the time series and high uncertainty.
